name: 1979 Constitutional Assembly Election
description: The election for the [Assembly of Experts for the Constitution](/orgs/assembly-of-experts-for-constitution) was held on 3–4 August 1979, six months after the [Iranian Revolution](/events/iranian-revolution-1979). Voters chose 73 members to draft the new constitution. Of 428 registered candidates, the [Islamic Republican Party](/orgs/islamic-republican-party) and Khomeini-aligned clerics won approximately 55 seats. Turnout was 51.7% (10.8 million of 20.9 million eligible voters). The [Freedom Movement of Iran](/orgs/freedom-movement-of-iran) and secular nationalists won a small minority of seats. [Abdul Rahman Ghassemlou](/people/abdul-rahman-ghassemlou), leader of the [Democratic Party of Iranian Kurdistan](/orgs/democratic-party-of-iranian-kurdistan), won over 80% of the vote in West Azerbaijan but his credentials were rejected on [Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini)'s orders.

sources.title: The 1979 Assembly of Experts for the Drafting of the Constitution Election

editorial_notes: 73 seats: 69 provincial + 4 religious minority. IRP won ~55 seats. 15 ayatollahs, 40 hojjat al-Islams elected. Top vote-getter: Mahmoud Taleghani (2,016,801 in Tehran).
